language.heading: Language
language.description: Choose the language used across the app.

hero.tagline: A chat platform built for real communities.
hero.about_body: Chat shouldn't come with a government ID check. In 2026, Discord began rolling out global age assurance — face scans and ID uploads handled by third-party vendors — just to access age-gated channels, tweak message-request settings, or prove you're an adult. After fierce backlash, the global launch was pushed to late 2026, but the UK, Australia, and Brazil are already live, with Europe and several US states close behind. A breach the prior October had already leaked users' government IDs from one of Discord's contractors. That's a broken deal. A community platform shouldn't be a gateway to your passport, your face, or a vendor's data-retention policy. Banter is built free, end-to-end encrypted, and anonymous by design. No face scans. No ID scans. No silent age-inference models profiling your account. Just people talking — the way the internet used to work.

downloads.heading: Get Banter for your desktop
downloads.subheading: A native client that keeps your session encrypted locally and feels right at home in your taskbar.
downloads.windows_note: Windows 10 or later. SmartScreen may warn until the publisher is established.
downloads.more_platforms: macOS and Linux builds coming soon.

subtitle.register: Pick a username and password to get started.
subtitle.forgot: Enter your username, upload your keyfile, and choose a new password.

register.agreement_prefix: By creating an account, you agree to our

keyfile.new_device_prompt: New device detected. Upload your keyfile to verify this is you.

forgot.default_response: If that account exists with a keyfile, the password has been reset.

keyfile.title: Keyfile
keyfile.explainer: Your keyfile is required when you log in from a new device and when changing your password. Generate one to enable two-factor recovery; rotate it any time (only your password is required, so this is also how you recover if you've lost the file); remove it only if you want to turn the second factor off entirely.
keyfile.rotate_explainer: This invalidates the old keyfile and generates a new one. Use this if you've lost the old file or think it may be compromised. Only your password is required.
keyfile.remove_explainer: Without a keyfile, anyone who learns your password can take over your account immediately. There is no second line of defence.

warning.title: Read this. We mean it.
warning.no_email: Banter does not collect your email or phone number. There is no recovery email and no SMS reset.
warning.no_support: Banter staff cannot reset your account. There is no support team that can bypass the keyfile. We do not have the ability — there is no back door, by design.
warning.no_backup: If you lose both your password and your keyfile, your account is gone forever. Your messages, your guilds, your friends list — gone.
warning.attacker: If an attacker obtains both your password and your keyfile (e.g. malware on your computer, or you upload them to a phishing site), they own the account. You cannot get it back. We cannot help.
warning.ack: I understand. I take full responsibility for storing my keyfile and password securely. There is no recovery.

developers.list_subtitle: Each application has one paired bot user.
developers.name_hint: 2-32 chars, no spaces. Becomes your bot's username.
developers.list_empty: No applications yet. Create one to get started.
developers.general_subtitle: Edit the public-facing information for your bot.
developers.placeholder_description: A short description of what your bot does — shown on the OAuth2 consent screen and in guild member lists.
developers.description_hint: Shown on the invite consent screen
developers.connect_body_prefix: Use the token you received at creation (or rotated from Danger Zone) with your bot library. Send it via the
developers.connect_body_oauth2: See the OAuth2 tab to generate an invite URL to add your bot to a server.
developers.oauth2_subtitle: Share this URL with server owners to add your bot. They'll pick which server to join, and the bot gets the permissions you select below.
developers.danger_subtitle: Irreversible actions. Think before you click.
developers.danger_rotate_body: Invalidates the current token immediately. Any running bot instance using the old token will disconnect.
developers.danger_delete_body: Destroys the app, its bot user, and all its guild memberships. Messages the bot sent stay in place as orphaned content.
developers.rotate_confirm_body: The current token stops working immediately. Update any running bots with the new token.
developers.delete_confirm_body: This cannot be undone. The bot will be kicked from every server and its token invalidated.
developers.token_modal_subtitle: Copy this now — it will not be shown again.
developers.token_modal_body: Store this securely in your bot's config file. If you lose it, rotate the token.
developers.display_name_hint: Shown in messages and member lists. Defaults to the bot name if empty.
developers.bio_hint: Shown on the bot's user profile card.
developers.commands_subtitle: Commands this bot has registered with the server.
developers.commands_empty_hint: Use bot.slash_command(...) in your SDK and the bot will sync them on next connect.
